phase3_findings.description: GIT_WORK_TREE changes pathspec resolution for a relative-path git command run from a subdirectory inside the Run Claude Code step

fix_decisions.claim_text: changeset trailer cites the wrong number (#643 vs #602)
fix_decisions.evidence: false positive: .changeset/README.md mandates PR-citation ('cite the PR number (#123)'); #643 is this branch's PR, so (#643) is correct per convention. 3 agents assumed issue-citation; the final-pass agent verified #643 is the PR.

parking_evidence.basis: the changeset citation convention is PR-cited per .changeset/README.md, and #643 is this branch's PR
parking_evidence.failing_input: the (#643) trailer, asserted to be a wrong issue reference
parking_evidence.source: .changeset/README.md ('cite the PR number (#123)') + gh pr view 643 == this branch

fix_decisions.claim_text: GIT_WORK_TREE redirects relative-path git ops run from a subdirectory
fix_decisions.evidence: the finder graded it MEDIUM but explicitly called it 'a documentation/awareness gap, not a code bug'; DevFlow's own git ops run from github.workspace (== GIT_WORK_TREE), so git add -A / commit / push behave identically (the issue's Desired Behavior). The subdir-relative-pathspec edge case does not arise in DevFlow's root-anchored usage. AC3's post-merge Linux end-to-end run confirms checkout/commit/push live.

parking_evidence.basis: GIT_WORK_TREE equals the agent's actual cwd (github.workspace); DevFlow anchors git ops to the repo root, so no subdir relative-pathspec op occurs; live confirmation is AC3 (post-merge)
parking_evidence.failing_input: a relative-path git command run from a subdirectory with GIT_WORK_TREE set
parking_evidence.source: issue #602 Desired Behavior + AC3; phase-1-setup.md anchors via git rev-parse --show-toplevel

shadow.reason: clean APPROVE at iteration 1 with zero fixes applied; the primary Phase-3 fan-out (5 agents) plus 4 /simplify cleanup agents provided the independent review. No fix-loop collusion to shadow-audit (0 fixes). A separate full shadow fan-out was not dispatched; coverage recorded not_verified honestly.

loop_verdict_reason: clean APPROVE (no Critical/Important code defect; the one Important-graded finding was self-downgraded by its finder to a non-code advisory caveat, benign for DevFlow's root-anchored git ops and covered by AC3 post-merge; the changeset finding is a refuted false positive). Shadow coverage not_verified (no separate fan-out; 0 fixes applied).
